items.headline: 20-unit apartment building permitted on Page Street
items.summary: A new 20-unit multifamily building receives a permit at 426 Page St in a $3.8 million project. This is the only new apartment construction in this permit batch, adding rental housing to the neighborhood.

items.headline: Santa Clara renews tourism improvement district for 2027-28
items.summary: City Council held a public hearing on renewing the Santa Clara Tourism Improvement District and approved an agreement with Discover Santa Clara to administer it. The council also took action on proposed Stadium Authority capital projects that had been deferred from the June 9 meeting.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study Housing Element update
items.summary: The Planning Commission scheduled a study session on the Housing Element update, which will address how Cupertino plans for housing. The Housing Element is a state-required document that sets local housing goals and strategies.

items.headline: New restaurant 'Ribbit' gets sign permit on University Avenue
items.summary: A new business called Ribbit has received approval to install an illuminated exterior sign in downtown Palo Alto's commercial district, suggesting a restaurant or food concept is moving into 364 University Avenue.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study artificial turf policy
items.summary: The Planning Commission scheduled a study session on June 22 to examine Sunnyvale's artificial turf policy. No details on scope, proposed changes, or timeline were provided in the agenda.

items.headline: Council to review zoning and protections for parks and recreational sites
items.summary: The City Council scheduled a study session to examine current designations, zoning rules, and state law protections affecting Cupertino's recreational facilities and parkland. The session will consider whether existing zoning and protections are adequate.
